stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo shares surged as the go-to-market intelligence platform continues to rebound from multi-year lows. The stock has been volatile within a wide 52-week range of $5.15 to $12.51, and today's move appears driven by speculative buying and short covering as the broader tech selloff creates rotation opportunities into beaten-down software names.

stocks.name: Netflix Inc.
stocks.explanation: Netflix dropped sharply after its Q2 earnings report disappointed on forward guidance. While revenue rose 13% year-over-year, Q3 revenue guidance of $12.86 billion came in below the ~$13 billion analyst consensus. The company also announced plans to reduce the frequency of its detailed viewing reports, raising concerns about viewer engagement trends.

stocks.name: Cadence Design Systems
stocks.explanation: Cadence fell steeply as EDA stocks were caught in the broader semiconductor selloff triggered by TSMC's capex hike and investor fatigue with chip-sector valuations. Cadence trades at a P/E of approximately 87.5x versus a peer average of 37.8x, making it particularly vulnerable to sentiment shifts ahead of its July 27 earnings report.

stocks.name: Synopsys Inc.
stocks.explanation: Synopsys declined alongside its EDA peer Cadence in the global chip selloff. The stock has pulled back roughly 33% from its two-year high as investors question stretched valuations at a P/E of ~112x. Synopsys also recently announced plans to discontinue certain manufacturing software products to pivot engineering resources toward AI-focused chip design.

stocks.name: Robinhood Markets
stocks.explanation: Robinhood extended its weekly decline of approximately 9% as shares retreated toward the critical $100 support level. Investor sentiment was weighed down by the company's planned $500 million asset-backed securities sale to fund consumer finance expansion, concerns about credit risk from its new premium credit card, and insider selling activity.

stocks.name: PubMatic Inc.
stocks.explanation: PubMatic fell in sympathy with the broader tech selloff as small-cap ad-tech names faced selling pressure. The programmatic advertising platform has been under pressure amid ongoing concerns about digital advertising spending growth and competition from larger platforms integrating AI-driven ad optimization.

stocks.name: Applied Materials
stocks.explanation: Applied Materials was hit hard in the global semiconductor equipment selloff following TSMC's capex guidance hike. As a major supplier to Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron, and Intel, AMAT is highly exposed to shifts in memory and AI capex sentiment. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF dropped nearly 4% as investors questioned the sustainability of infrastructure spending.

stocks.name: Upstart Holdings
stocks.explanation: Upstart declined amid the broader tech selloff as risk appetite contracted across growth and fintech names. The AI lending platform remains volatile and sensitive to macro sentiment shifts, particularly around interest rate expectations and consumer credit conditions.

stocks.name: Flex Ltd.
stocks.explanation: Flex fell as the electronics manufacturing services provider was swept into the semiconductor supply chain selloff. As a contract manufacturer serving major chip and hardware companies, Flex's stock is closely correlated with semiconductor sector sentiment and concerns about the pace of AI infrastructure buildout.

stocks.name: Onto Innovation
stocks.explanation: Onto Innovation dropped alongside chip equipment peers in the TSMC-driven selloff. The semiconductor process control and inspection equipment maker is directly exposed to wafer fab capital spending trends, and investors sold chip equipment names broadly after TSMC raised its capex forecast while margins came under scrutiny.

stocks.name: XPeng Inc.
stocks.explanation: XPeng declined as Chinese tech ADRs faced broad selling pressure amid the global risk-off session. The EV maker also faces headwinds from intensifying competition in the Chinese electric vehicle market and uncertainty around global trade dynamics following Xi Jinping's comments on AI cooperation at the Shanghai WAIC summit.

stocks.name: Pure Storage
stocks.explanation: Pure Storage bucked the broader tech selloff as the all-flash storage provider benefited from continued enterprise demand for AI-optimized data infrastructure. The company's Q1 revenue of $778.5M beat estimates, and analysts maintain a Buy consensus with a $90 median price target, reflecting confidence in Pure's positioning for AI workload storage needs.

stocks.name: Sea Limited
stocks.explanation: Sea Limited fell alongside broader tech and emerging market names in the global risk-off move. The Singapore-based e-commerce and gaming conglomerate is sensitive to Asian market sentiment, which was particularly negative after TSMC's selloff triggered a correction in Taiwan and broader Asia-Pacific tech indices.

stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Wolfspeed declined as the silicon carbide semiconductor maker was caught in the broader chip selloff. The company continues to navigate a challenging transition to larger wafer sizes and scaling its Mohawk Valley fab, making it more vulnerable to negative sentiment in semiconductor capital spending cycles.

stocks.name: Lattice Semiconductor
stocks.explanation: Lattice Semiconductor fell in the global chip selloff as the FPGA maker's exposure to industrial and automotive end markets—sectors that Synopsys management described as 'fairly muted'—added to the negative pressure from broader semiconductor sentiment following TSMC's results.
